architecture.summary: DSR models fake news detection as a three-agent dialectical reasoning system over multimodal social context. A Verifier agent constructs evidence paths supporting semantic consistency; a Debunker agent actively explores logical or factual contradictions; a differentiable Judge agent integrates local evidence with global parametric knowledge to adjudicate between competing perspectives, producing both a classification and dialectically grounded explanations.

biblio.title: Dialectical Structured Reasoning for Explainable Multimodal Fake News Detection

claims.statement: DSR achieves state-of-the-art performance on three benchmarks
DSR produces transparent, dialectically grounded explanations that closely mirror human reasoning process
Current multimodal fake news detectors function as opaque classifiers, offering limited deductive transparency and little insight into how conflicting evidence is reconciled
